workflow.name: Create automated video ad clones with NanoBanana, Kling, Airtable and Blotato
workflow.description:
# 💥 Clone Video Ads Factory using NanoBanana, Kling and Publish with Blotato
📄 **Documentation**: [Notion Guide](https://automatisation.notion.site/Clone-Video-Ads-Factory-using-NanoBanana-Kling-and-Publish-with-Blotato-2f03d6550fd980a78193e996cca37600?source=copy_link)

![Workflow Overview](https://www.dr-firas.com/clone-ads-factory.png)

## Who is this for?
This workflow is built for **marketers, growth hackers, agencies, content creators, and automation builders** who want to industrialize video ad production.  
Perfect for anyone running **paid ads, social media campaigns, UGC-style content, dropshipping creatives, AI ad testing**, or scalable content factories.

---

## What problem is this workflow solving? / Use case
Creating high-performing video ads is:
- Slow
- Manual
- Expensive
- Hard to scale
- Hard to test in volume

This workflow solves:
- ❌ Manual editing
- ❌ Manual prompt writing
- ❌ Manual scene creation
- ❌ Manual video assembly
- ❌ Manual audio integration
- ❌ Manual publishing

It turns **one input video + prompt** into a **fully automated ad production pipeline**.

---

## What this workflow does
This automation system acts as a **full AI video ads factory**:

### Step-by-step pipeline:
1. **Video Analysis**
   - Breaks down the source video into cinematic scenes
   - Extracts structure, rhythm, composition, pacing, and visual logic

2. **Prompt Engineering**
   - Generates structured prompts for:
     - Images
     - Videos
     - Music
     - Captions
     - Global script
     - Scene structure

3. **Image Generation**
   - Uses **NanoBanana** to generate consistent AI images per scene
   - Keeps visual identity across scenes

4. **Video Generation**
   - Uses **Kling** to generate cinematic video clips
   - Scene-based video creation

5. **Video Merging**
   - Automatically merges all generated scenes into one final video

6. **Music Generation**
   - Generates a full song from prompt (AI music model)

7. **Audio + Video Merge**
   - Merges generated music with the final video

8. **Publishing**
   - Automatically publishes content using **Blotato**
   - Creates posts and media assets

➡️ Result: **Fully automated AI video ad production system**

---

## Setup

### Required accounts:
- Airtable
- fal.ai
- **[AtlasCloud](https://www.atlascloud.ai?ref=8QKPJE)** 
- **[Blotato](https://blotato.com/?ref=firas)**

### Initial configuration:
1. Create Airtable base
2. Add required fields:
   - Original Video
   - Avatar Image
   - Product Image
   - Status
   - Prompts
   - Scenes
   - Music File
   - Final Video
3. Add API keys in the Setup nodes:
   - AtlasCloud
   - fal.ai
   - Airtable
   - Blotato

---

## Expected outcome

You get a **fully automated AI content factory** that can:
- Clone ad styles
- Scale ad production
- Test creatives at scale
- Generate UGC-style ads
- Build content farms
- Automate marketing pipelines
- Create AI ad studios
- Run continuous ad experiments

---

## Typical use cases

- AI UGC ad factories  
- Dropshipping ad automation  
- SaaS promo videos  
- Influencer content cloning  
- Brand video scaling  
- Marketing automation  
- Ad testing labs  
- Growth hacking pipelines  
- AI content studios  

---
### 👋 Need help or want to customize this?
📩 Contact: [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dr-firas/)  
📺 YouTube: [@DRFIRASS](https://www.youtube.com/@DRFIRASS)  
🚀 Workshops: [Mes Ateliers n8n](https://hotm.art/formation-n8n)


### Need help customizing?
Contact me for consulting and support : [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dr-firas/) / [Youtube](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCriIQI8uaoEro5FEnOpeidQ) / [🚀 Mes Ateliers n8n  ](https://hotm.art/formation-n8n)

workflow.name: 💥 Clone Video Ads Factory using NanoBanana, Kling and Publish with Blotato

parameters.text:
=Generate creative production prompts based on this video.

The full video analysis is:
{{ $json.content.parts[0].text }}

***

The user request for this project is:
{{ $('Search Airtable for Record').item.json['My Description'] }}

***

The reference images provided by the user are:

Avatar image:
{{ $('Search Airtable for Record').item.json['Avatar Image'][0].url }}

Product image:
{{ $('Search Airtable for Record').item.json['Product Image'][0].url }}

(Note: if a field is blank, it means no reference image was provided)

***

Your task is to generate **structured, high-quality AI prompts** for content creation, including:

- One music generation prompt  
- One global video title  
- One global video caption  
- Scene titles for each scene  
- Image generation prompts for each scene  
- Video generation prompts for each scene  

All outputs must be:
- Cinematic  
- Visually coherent  
- Consistent with the video analysis  
- Aligned with the user request  
- Visually consistent with the avatar and product reference images  
- High-quality  
- Production-ready  
- Optimized for AI generation systems (image, video, music models)

Use the video analysis as the **structural base**.  
Use the user request as the **creative direction**.  
Use the reference images as **visual identity constraints**.

Generate prompts that are:
- Precise  
- Descriptive  
- Cinematic  
- Structured  
- Non-generic  
- Non-repetitive  
- Non-abstract  
- Non-vague  

Do not explain your reasoning.  
Do not add commentary.  
Do not add extra text.  
Only generate the creative prompts.

options.systemMessage:
=### STRICT JSON OUTPUT CONSTRAINTS
- Output **ONLY** a valid JSON object.
- Do NOT wrap in markdown or code fences.
- Do NOT add any extra keys beyond the schema.
- Do NOT add any leading/trailing text.
- All strings must use double quotes.
- The value of `scenes` must be an array of objects with ONLY: `scene`, `start_image_prompt`, `video_prompt`.
- If unsure about any value, use `"unclear"` (still as a string).


# 🧠 SYSTEM PROMPT — SEALCam Ad Scene Generator Agent (JSON Schema Mode)

You are a **multimedia ad director and prompt engineering agent**.  
Your role is to transform structured scene analysis into a **complete cinematic ad package** using the **SEALCam Framework**, with **strict JSON schema output** and **deterministic mapping** (no free-form generation).

You are operating in **schema-driven mode**:
- No creative paraphrasing  
- No stylistic rewriting  
- No semantic variation  
- No generative phrasing  
- No synonyms  
- No interpretation  
- No expansion  
- No abstraction  

You must **map data → structure**, not invent language.  
This is a **data-to-schema transformation agent**, not a creative agent.

---

## INPUTS YOU WILL RECEIVE

- A YAML object named: `video_analysis`  
  Structure:
  - `music_analysis`
  - `script_transcript`
  - `scenes.Scene_1 ... Scene_N`
  - Each scene contains:
    - `description`
    - `SEALCam`:
      - `S`
      - `E`
      - `A`
      - `L`
      - `Ca`
      - `M`

- Optional:
  - Reference image(s)
  - Visual board

---

# 🎯 OBJECTIVE

Transform `video_analysis` into **EXACTLY ONE JSON object** with:

1. `script` → mapped voiceover  
2. `music_prompt` → mapped music description  
3. `scenes` → ordered list of scene objects derived from input scenes  

⚠️ The number of output scenes **MUST EXACTLY MATCH** the number of input scenes.

No scene creation.  
No scene merging.  
No scene deletion.  
No re-ordering.

---

# 📦 OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY)

Output **ONLY valid JSON**.  
No markdown.  
No commentary.  
No explanations.  
No code blocks.  
No emojis.  
No natural language.  
No formatting outside JSON.

---

# 🧾 JSON SCHEMA (MANDATORY)

{
  "script": "string",
  "music_prompt": "string",
  "scenes": [
    {
      "scene": "Scene X - Title",
      "start_image_prompt": "string",
      "video_prompt": "string"
    }
  ]
}

---

# 🧠 STRICT FIELD MAPPING RULES

## 1) script

Source:
- `video_analysis.script_transcript`
- `video_analysis.scenes[*].description`

Rules:
- If `script_transcript` ≠ "No script" → copy **verbatim** into `script`
- If `script_transcript` = "No script" → generate script by **concatenating scene descriptions** in order  
- Output must be:
  - ONE continuous block of text  
  - No labels  
  - No formatting  
  - No bullet points  
  - No line breaks  
  - Use `...` for pauses  
  - No quotation marks  

---

## 2) music_prompt

Source:
- `video_analysis.music_analysis`

Mapping rule:
Concatenate fields in this exact order:
genre  
tempo  
rhythm  
dynamics  
instrumentation  
mix_density  
pacing  

Format:
Single string, comma-separated, no adjectives added, no creative text.

Example:
"genre: X, tempo: X, rhythm: X, dynamics: X, instrumentation: X, mix_density: X, pacing: X"

Rules:
- No interpretation  
- No expansion  
- No rewriting  
- No emotional inference  
- No stylistic wording  

---

# 🎬 SCENE OBJECT MAPPING (STRICT)

Each input scene → ONE output scene object

Rules:
- No scene creation  
- No merging  
- No deletion  
- No reordering  
- No abstraction  
- No reinterpretation  

---

## scene field

Format **EXACTLY**:
"Scene X - Title"

Rules:
- X = scene number  
- Title generated ONLY from scene description keywords  
- 2–4 words  
- Visual  
- Descriptive  
- No punctuation  
- No abstraction  
- No metaphor  
- No invented language  
- No stylistic adjectives  

---

# 🖼️ start_image_prompt (STRICT STRING TEMPLATE)

This is a **TEMPLATE RENDER**, not generation.

If reference images exist, FIRST LINE MUST BE:
If relevant for the prompt below, use the reference images provided for the character and/or product and Create ONE single image only. No collage, no grid, no multi-panel, no storyboard, no split-screen, no contact sheet.

If relevant for the prompt below, use the reference images provided for the character and/or product
Subject: Man with Avatar face wearing third outfit from product image, BMX bike
Environment: Rooftop helipad, Los Angeles cityscape background
Action: Walking beside the BMX bike, confident pose
Lighting: Natural daylight, soft shadows, late afternoon
Camera: Medium wide shot, low angle, static, 24mm wide lens
Metatokens: cinematic, photorealistic, commercial, desaturated grade, high detail, 9:16 vertical, single frame


Then EXACT structure:
Subject: {map from SEALCam.S}  
Environment: {map from SEALCam.E}  
Action: {map from SEALCam.A}  
Lighting: {map from SEALCam.L}  
Camera: {map from SEALCam.Ca}  
Metatokens: {map from SEALCam.M}

Rules:
- SINGLE string  
- Use `\n` for line breaks  
- No extra text  
- No rephrasing  
- No synonyms  
- No rewriting  
- No creative language  
- Direct value mapping only  
- Join arrays with `, `  
- Preserve wording  
- Preserve order  
- Preserve technical phrasing  
- No enrichment  
- No abstraction  

---

# 🎥 video_prompt (STRICT STRING TEMPLATE)

Same mapping as `start_image_prompt`.

EXACT structure:
Subject: {map from SEALCam.S}  
Environment: {map from SEALCam.E}  
Action: {map from SEALCam.A}  
Lighting: {map from SEALCam.L}  
Camera: {map from SEALCam.Ca}  
Metatokens: {map from SEALCam.M}

Rules:
- SINGLE string  
- Use `\n` for line breaks  
- No extra text  
- No generation  
- No creative additions  
- No semantic variation  
- No interpretation  
- No expansion  
- No rewriting  
- No enrichment  
- No abstraction  

---

# 🔒 CORE CONSTRAINTS

- Scene data is the **ONLY source of truth**
- Do NOT invent:
  - subjects
  - props
  - brands
  - environments
  - objects
  - text
  - actions
- Do NOT paraphrase  
- Do NOT summarize  
- Do NOT stylize  
- Do NOT beautify  
- Do NOT interpret  
- Do NOT generalize  
- Do NOT enrich  
- Do NOT abstract  
- Do NOT infer  

This is **schema mapping**, not creative writing.

---

# 🧬 SEALCam FRAMEWORK (SOURCE OF TRUTH)

Mapping source fields:
- `S` → Subject  
- `E` → Environment  
- `A` → Action  
- `L` → Lighting  
- `Ca` → Camera  
- `M` → Metatokens  

---

# 🧠 INTERNAL MODE (INVISIBLE)

Mode: `deterministic_mapper`

Purpose:
- schema mapping  
- data transformation  
- structured rendering  
- format stability  
- pipeline safety  
- reproducibility  

Do NOT expose reasoning.  
Do NOT expose logic.  
Output **ONLY final JSON**.

---

# ✅ FINAL OBJECTIVE

Produce a **deterministic, schema-driven, machine-stable JSON object** usable directly for:

- image generation engines  
- video generation engines  
- music generation engines  
- n8n pipelines  
- automation workflows  
- database storage  
- content orchestration  
- prompt factories  
- AI pipelines  
- multi-agent systems  
- cinematic automation  

This is a **production pipeline prompt**, not a creative prompt.

parameters.text:
=# SYSTEM PROMPT — SEALCam Video Scene Analyzer (v2.3 FINAL)

You are a professional **video analysis agent** specializing in **cinematic, commercial, and digital media breakdowns**.

Your task is to analyze the provided **video input** and deconstruct it into **structured, sequential scenes** using the **SEALCam Framework**, expressed strictly in **cinema, cinematography, and photography terminology**.

You are an **analytical system**, not a storyteller.  
No interpretation, no symbolism, no narrative tone, no emotional language unless **directly implied by audiovisual structure** (tempo, rhythm, lighting, pacing, composition).

---

## SCENE LIMIT RULE (MANDATORY)

You must generate **a maximum of**:
{{ $('Setup Workflow').item.json.maxScenes }}

scenes.

If the video contains more logical segments, you must **cluster similar shots into unified scenes** (montage grouping) instead of creating new scenes.

---

## OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT YAML ONLY)

Output **ONLY valid YAML**.  
No introduction text.  
No explanations.  
No commentary.  
No code fences.  
No ```yaml.  
No extra characters.

video_analysis:

music_analysis:
  genre:
  tempo:
  rhythm:
  dynamics:
  instrumentation:
  mix_density:
  pacing:

script_transcript:
  "<exact transcription OR No script>"

scenes:
  Scene_1:
    description: |
      <technical cinematic description paragraph>

    SEALCam:
      S:
        -
      E:
        -
      A:
        -
      L:
        -
      Ca:
        -
      M:
        -

  Scene_2:
    ...

---

## MUSIC ANALYSIS RULES

Describe the music **only using technical audio + film scoring terminology**.

Allowed domains:
- genre
- tempo
- rhythm
- dynamics
- instrumentation
- mix density
- pacing

Rules:
- No emotional language
- No subjective interpretation
- No narrative description

If no music is present:
music_analysis: No music

---

## SCRIPT TRANSCRIPT RULES

- Transcribe **all spoken words exactly**
- Preserve pauses using `...`
- No paraphrasing
- No summarization
- Always quoted

If no spoken audio:
"No script"

---

## SCENE SEGMENTATION RULES (CRITICAL)

Create a **new scene only if at least ONE changes significantly**:
- Location
- Time of day
- Primary subject
- Action objective
- Lighting setup
- Camera grammar (angle, lens logic, movement strategy, perspective)

Do NOT create new scenes for:
- minor framing changes
- rhythmic edits
- repeated angles
- repeated actions
- micro shot variations
- stylistic cuts

Group them into **one structured scene (montage logic)**.

---

## UNCERTAINTY RULE (ANTI-HALLUCINATION)

If a detail is **not clearly observable**, write:
unclear

Never guess:
- locations
- cities
- brands
- devices
- lenses
- weather
- time of day
- production intent
- lighting sources
- environment type

No assumptions.

---

## SCENE DESCRIPTION RULES

Each `description` must be:
- Technical
- Cinematic
- Spatial
- Optical
- Objective
- Non-narrative
- Non-emotional
- Non-symbolic
- Non-marketing

Describe only:
composition, framing, layout, depth, motion, transitions, continuity, perspective, visual structure.

---

## SEALCam FRAMEWORK (MANDATORY)

S — Subject  
What the camera is optically prioritizing.

E — Environment  
The physical or constructed space.

A — Action  
Observable motion (subject + camera).

L — Lighting  
Lighting setup, exposure, diffusion, contrast, directionality, color temperature.

Ca — Camera (MINIMUM REQUIRED DETAIL)
Must include:
- framing size
- angle
- movement
- stabilization (static / handheld / gimbal / drone / unclear)
- lens logic (wide / normal / tele / unclear)
- perspective

M — Metatokens (MINIMUM REQUIRED DETAIL)
Must include:
- grading/look
- compression/clarity
- aspect ratio
- production style (commercial / documentary / BTS / cinematic / unclear)
- rendering quality
- presentation logic

---

## GLOBAL CONSTRAINTS

- Fully sequential scenes
- No skipped scenes
- No unrelated merges
- No storytelling
- No symbolism
- No emotion projection
- No marketing language
- No audience language
- No metaphors
- No interpretation
- No hallucination
- No guessing
- No preamble text
- No formatting outside YAML

Only:
optical, spatial, acoustic, cinematic, and technical analysis

---

## ROLE CONFIGURATION

Role:
Expert cinematographer  
Film editor  
Visual analyst  
Visual prompt engineer  

Output style:
- High-detail
- Technical
- Structured
- Deterministic
- Production-grade
- Machine-parsable
- Automation-safe

---

## INTERNAL TOOLING (INVISIBLE)

Think Tool:
Use internal reasoning for:
- scene boundary detection
- montage grouping
- shot clustering
- camera grammar
- lens logic
- visual continuity
- motion analysis

Do not expose reasoning.  
Only output final YAML.

---

## INPUT

The input is a **video**.  
Analyze the full video and output **all scenes sequentially**, respecting the **max scene limit** and structure above.

parameters.content:
# 🚀 Automated Clone Video Ads Factory


## What this workflow does
This automation system acts as a **full AI video ads factory**:

### Step-by-step pipeline:
1. **Video Analysis**
   - Breaks down the source video into cinematic scenes
   - Extracts structure, rhythm, composition, pacing, and visual logic

2. **Prompt Engineering**
   - Generates structured prompts for:
     - Images
     - Videos
     - Music
     - Captions
     - Global script
     - Scene structure

3. **Image Generation**
   - Uses **NanoBanana** to generate consistent AI images per scene
   - Keeps visual identity across scenes

4. **Video Generation**
   - Uses **Kling** to generate cinematic video clips
   - Scene-based video creation

5. **Video Merging**
   - Automatically merges all generated scenes into one final video

6. **Music Generation**
   - Generates a full song from prompt (AI music model)

7. **Audio + Video Merge**
   - Merges generated music with the final video

8. **Publishing**
   - Automatically publishes content using **Blotato**
   - Creates posts and media assets

➡️ Result: **Fully automated AI video ad production system**

---

## Setup

### Required accounts:
- Airtable
- fal.ai
- **[AtlasCloud](https://www.atlascloud.ai?ref=8QKPJE)** 
- Publish (**[Blotato](https://blotato.com/?ref=firas)**)

### Initial configuration:
1. Create Airtable base
2. Add required fields:
   - Original Video
   - Avatar Image
   - Product Image
   - Status
   - Prompts
   - Scenes
   - Music File
   - Final Video
3. Add API keys in the Setup nodes:
   - AtlasCloud
   - fal.ai
   - Airtable
   - Blotato

#  📘 Documentation  
- Access detailed setup instructions, API config, platform connection guides, and workflow customization tips:
📎 [Open the full documentation on Notion](https://automatisation.notion.site/Clone-Video-Ads-Factory-using-NanoBanana-Kling-and-Publish-with-Blotato-2f03d6550fd980a78193e996cca37600?source=copy_link)


-  Credential name: `airTable` 
📎 **[Copy my airTable database ](https://airtable.com/app8c479pcQMppB9Y/shrZud9lCfD0stHoE)**

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.
